title: Limping Human
moment: Sits on the edge of a toadstool with one boot off, sipping bitter tea from a leaf-cup. Shrugs and points at the foot: "just this."
fieldNotes: Human — baseline mortal humanoid: soft skin, red blood, no bark, horns, or innate season-magic. Ordinary organs and healing rates — the reference body against which fey anatomies are compared.
probA: Right foot sits wrong in the boot; bone grinds when he puts weight on it.
probB: A dark line is climbing the calf under the skin — cold to the touch, moving upward by the minute.
gm: A=SET/BRACE. B=DRAIN or CUT AWAY. Easy open; flare = infection line.

title: Fevered Pixie
moment: Curled in a thimble nest set on a toadstool — wings trembling, dust flashing gold then blue; trying (and failing) to drink from a dewdrop.
fieldNotes: Pixie — palm-scale winged fey; hollow bones, delicate wing membranes, often luminous dust on the wings. Tiny organ volumes — heat and fluid balance swing easily. Full person-scale mind in a thimble body.
probA: Fever swings hard — one moment scorching, next shivering; neither state holds.
probB: Wing-joints lock mid-beat; one wing sticks open, the other won’t extend.
gm: A=DRAIN/COOL. B=SET tiny joints. Small = easy to underestimate.

title: Bandaged Satyr
moment: Reclines on a soft-cap bed, chest wrapped, humming under his breath; a frost-lilac ribbon clenched in one fist, free hand tapping a silent rhythm.
fieldNotes: Satyr — fey with humanoid torso and goatlike legs/hooves; horns common, keen hearing. Dual gait (person above, ungulate below). Warm mammalian blood; gut suited to forage and wine as much as rations.
probA: Chest wrapping hides a seam that opens on every inhale; air whistles where it shouldn’t.
probB: Fingers of the left hand have gone stiff and wooden — freezing from the tips toward the palm.
gm: LOVER A · pair #07. A=SEAL. B=ADD warmth. Ribbon matches nymph.

title: Muddy Gnome
moment: Sits upright on a muddy cap, boots still dripping river-gray, trying to count coins with shaking hands.
fieldNotes: Gnome — small humanoid; stout bones, keen fine-motor nerves, warm red blood like mortals. Earth-attuned gut and skin — hardy, not plant-bodied.
probA: Leg wound packed with gray ford-mud; the dressing stays wet and the flesh underneath won’t clot.
probB: Whole body seizes in short, violent shakes whenever anyone says an odd number aloud nearby.
gm: SOLDIER (early). A=SEAL. B=STOP MOTION. Soft truth: odd counts.

title: Bitten Dryad
moment: Kneels carefully on a clean white-cap, arms held out so the cold-iron bites show; blue-black soil still under her claws. Watching the healers.
fieldNotes: Dryad — tree-bound fey; bark-and-leaf integument, sap for blood, body grown as much as born. Life force tethered to a living tree. Cold iron is toxic; plant chemistry, not mammalian clotting.
probA: Cold-iron tooth-heads are still lodged under the bark along both forearms.
probB: Sap-level is crashing — matte eyes, no give at the wrist, leaf-hair going brittle at the tips.
gm: A=EXTRACT. B=FILL/IV. Looks composed; sap crash is the fold.

title: Exhausted Brownie
moment: Collapsed face-down on a low cap mid-apology for "leaving the rest"; crushed hands tucked under their chin. Still trying to refuse a cup of broth.
fieldNotes: Brownie — ~2 ft household-and-road fey; plain-looking, tough for size, extreme manual dexterity. Warm blood, mortal-like organs scaled small. Built for long quiet labor.
probA: Both hands are crushed — bones like gravel under the skin; fingers will not open.
probB: They are running on nothing — pulse thready, lips gray; the body is about to stop volunteering.
gm: A=SET hands. B=ADD/FILL + rest. Collapse is the fold.

title: Burned Nymph
moment: Lies very still on a pale-cap, lilac-frost bandages neat; a Summer ribbon hidden in one fist. Eyes open, listening for one particular voice.
fieldNotes: Nymph — nature-beauty fey; soft-skinned, elflike frame, presence that can dazzle mortal senses when healthy. Flesh like fine mortal tissue; danger is often the aura and wild-place bond, not armored hide.
probA: Burn-cloth sticks to living skin; peeling any edge takes flesh with it.
probB: Under the wraps, patches of skin have gone ash-gray and hard — dead plates spreading at the edges.
gm: LOVER B · pair #03. A=careful dress. B=CUT AWAY dead. Urgent face.

title: Cracked Spriggan
moment: Half-sitting on a wide cap in Summer levy colors; ribs split like a dry log, honey-sap pooling under him. Eyes open; not talking.
fieldNotes: Spriggan — gnome-descended fey warped by old magic. Natural form ~3 ft, thick-bodied and foul-smelling; can swell to giant size. Same organs, violently redistributed mass — denser and oilier than ordinary gnomes.
probA: Rib-bark is cracked wide; the chest wall flexes wrong and won’t take a breath deep.
probB: Honey-thick sap pours from the splits faster than it should — sticky, sweet, pooling under him.
gm: SOLDIER. A=BRACE. B=SEAL. Courts left them.

title: Split Centaur
moment: Sprawled across two toadstool beds lashed together — split fore-hoof dangling, ears pinned flat whenever the ward gets loud. Gnawing a dry oatcake and glaring.
fieldNotes: Centaur — single creature, dual architecture: humanoid torso fused to equine trunk and four legs. Two weight systems, one bloodstream. Digestion and balance horse-forward; hands and speech person-forward.
probA: Fore-hoof is split lengthwise; the two halves grind and catch on every shift of weight.
probB: Every loud sound triggers a bright nosebleed and a blackout flicker behind the eyes.
gm: A=BRACE hoof. B=quiet ward / DRAIN head pressure.

title: Scorched Quickling
moment: Keeps skidding off his toadstool — scorched, twitching, reeking of ozone and clove. No name left to give.
fieldNotes: Quickling — ~2 ft slender fey; pale skin, feral features. Metabolism and nerves run far hotter than mortal norms — short natural lifespan; speech and motion often too fast to parse unless they deliberately slow.
probA: Veins strobe with leftover Pale Ring charge; sparks jump to metal nearby.
probB: Chest hitches in uneven, skipping heaves — long pauses, then double-thumps.
gm: DART · EMP module. A=DRAIN charge. B=RESTORE RHYTHM (defib spikes charge).

title: Soaked Kelpie
moment: Sits bolt upright on a wet cap (won’t lie down), levy sash still on, dripping riverwater, kicking anyone who offers a towel.
fieldNotes: Kelpie — aquatic shapeshifter; horse-form and person-form share one wet anatomy. Tissue stays damp; can hold and move water through lungs and hide. Predatory fey by nature.
probA: Lungs full of black river-water; every cough brings more; they will not lie flat.
probB: One leg is twisted wrong from the haul out of the ford — joint will not take weight.
gm: SOLDIER. A=DRAIN lungs. B=BRACE leg. Won’t recline.

title: Pierced Eladrin
moment: Propped against the stalk of his bed-cap — two shafts in a wrong-bent shoulder, pupils blown, jaw locked in a grin that won’t quit.
fieldNotes: Eladrin — Feywild elf-kin; tall, sharp-eared, long-lived. Blood and mood carry seasonal court magic. Soft tissue like elves; cold iron irritates; emotion and physiology couple tightly.
probA: Two arrow shafts sit deep in the shoulder; the joint is bent the wrong way around them.
probB: He cannot stop laughing — hard, silent convulsions that bounce the shafts and tear the wound wider.
gm: SOLDIER. A=EXTRACT. B=STOP MOTION. Only eladrin.

title: Empty Bee-kin
moment: Sits stiff on a honey-stained cap — empty armor plates, no hum. Proud eyes open; keeps picking at their own wrists as if calling something home.
fieldNotes: Bee-kin — fey whose outer plates are living hive-cells — chitin architecture with a colony interface, soft body beneath. Healthy specimens hum; empty or collapsing cells stress the whole organism.
probA: Hive-armor cells are vacant and collapsing inward, pinching the body underneath.
probB: Honey-fever burns behind the eyes; they keep stinging their own wrists calling a swarm that does not answer.
gm: Late but Delayed (not all red). A=FILL/brace cells. B=COOL fever.

title: Rooted Wood Woad
moment: Keeps tipping off its bed-cap — market twine still knotted around one wrist. Knothole eyes track whoever gets close; body won’t stay still.
fieldNotes: Wood Woad — plant guardian in humanoid shape; thick bark, branch-hands, root-feet, deep-set eyes. No ordinary animal heart — sustains on sun, soil, water, and air. True plant tissue.
probA: Tendril-arm almost severed — only a few living fibers still connect it to the shoulder.
probB: Whole torso jolts in hard spasms; it cannot hold still long enough for anyone to work.

title: Winded Pooka
moment: Keeps flickering between rabbit-ears and a lanky youth on the same toadstool — laughing wrong, then choking mid-laugh. Hay stuck in wet fur.
fieldNotes: Pooka — shapeshifting trickster fey; common forms include black horse, goat, or oversized rabbit-headed youth. One body, many silhouettes; lungs and throat reconfigure mid-shift. Mischief is metabolism.
probA: Stuck mid-shift — throat is half-hare, half-person; air whistles and catches.
probB: Whenever someone nearby lies, they begin to drown on dry air — panic, blue lips, no water in sight.
gm: BACKUP. A=BRACE/settle form. B=truth-quiet or DRAIN panic. Only pooka.

title: Bleached Huldra
moment: Sits sideways so no one sees her back; bark-hollow showing under a torn cloak. Cow-tail lashing. Smile too wide for the pain.
fieldNotes: Huldra — forest seducer fey; human-beautiful from the front, hollow or bark-backed behind, often with a cow’s tail. Soft mortal-facing flesh; the hollow is the true vulnerability.
probA: The hollow in her back is packing with dry rot — edges crumbling inward when she breathes.
probB: Tail has gone dead-weight and ice-cold; frost is climbing the spine from the tip.
gm: BACKUP. A=SEAL/FILL hollow. B=WARMTH. Do not force her to turn.

title: Snared Twigjack
moment: Tangled in its own briar-snare on a low cap — tiny, furious, picking at wire-thorns with stick fingers. Spits seeds at anyone who leans in.
fieldNotes: Twigjack — tiny plant-fey of briar and stick; sharp, springy, mean for its size. No red blood — sap and fiber. Built to snare, not to be snared.
probA: Own thorns are driven through both palms — stuck to the bed-cap by its own snare.
probB: Seed-spit becomes choking burrs in its own throat when it gets angry — and it is always angry.
gm: BACKUP · Delayed. A=EXTRACT thorns carefully. B=CLEAR airway / calm.

title: Hollow Sylph
moment: Hovers an inch above the toadstool, too light — translucent ribs showing, wind circling her like a worried pet. Speaks in gasps of cold air.
fieldNotes: Sylph — air-elemental fey; light bones, translucent skin, breath and breeze as one system. Needs moving air the way lungs need oxygen; still rooms starve them.
probA: She is too light — ribs show, lift is failing; she cannot stay grounded or truly fly.
probB: A dead still-pocket has formed in her chest; when the ward goes quiet, she begins to gray out.
gm: BACKUP. A=WEIGHT/anchor + FILL. B=keep air moving (fan, breath, Jubilee irony).
